input.prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic UGC realism. Same creator throughout: Maya, 44, medium-length dark brown hair in a low ponytail, minimal makeup, cream knit top and black leggings, visible on camera the entire clip. First 2 seconds are person-only framing: a soft handheld medium close-up in a dim early-morning kitchen as Maya pauses with one hand on the counter, looking slightly drained but composed. Then a slow lateral move follows her as she rubs her neck, glances at her phone, and exhales before heading toward the window. Natural cool dawn light with gentle shadows, premium home setting, muted neutral tones. Keep actions simple and realistic, no product visible yet, no text, no jumpy edits. The emotional beat is the quiet realization that her energy and recovery do not feel as easy as they used to. Keep the same named on-camera persona continuity across person-led clips. Start with person-only framing for the first 1.8 to 2.0 seconds. Realism directive: documentary-style photorealism; natural skin texture and pores, accurate facial anatomy and hand/finger proportions, physically plausible body motion and eye gaze, true-to-life fabric and hair behavior, realistic exposure and shadow roll-off, handheld camera micro-movement, and subtle real-world imperfections without beauty-filter look.
AUDIO: Calm trustworthy female narration, measured and clear: "After forty, the shift can feel subtle. Less steady energy. Slower recovery. Not dramatic—just harder to bounce back." Very light ambient piano with soft pulse. Natural room ambience: distant fridge hum, faint footsteps, quiet exhale, fabric movement. No hype, no rushed delivery.

Vertical 9:16 with strict continuity: same Maya, same age and appearance, now in the same home moments later with coherent wardrobe continuity. First 2 seconds are person-only framing: medium close-up at the kitchen island as she sets down a glass of water and looks calm, intentional. Then the camera performs one slow push-in as she reaches for a premium Bioluxe Lab NMN bottle placed naturally beside a simple breakfast with eggs, avocado, and yogurt. Show her opening the bottle, taking one capsule with water, then a believable improvement beat: later in the same clip she walks through bright morning light toward the front door with more upright posture and a small confident smile, gym tote in hand. Clean soft sunlight, realistic product handling, capsule visible, premium but authentic. Product label should remain clear and consistent with the reference image, integrated naturally into the scene. Keep the same named on-camera persona continuity across person-led clips. Start with person-only framing for the first 1.8 to 2.0 seconds. Realism directive: documentary-style photorealism; natural skin texture and pores, accurate facial anatomy and hand/finger proportions, physically plausible body motion and eye gaze, true-to-life fabric and hair behavior, realistic exposure and shadow roll-off, handheld camera micro-movement, and subtle real-world imperfections without beauty-filter look.
AUDIO: Calm expert narration synced to action: "Maya added one 500 milligram NMN capsule each morning—an easy way to support healthy NAD+ levels as part of her routine." Brief on-camera natural line from Maya, soft and genuine: "It just fits my mornings." Background music lifts slightly with warm piano and subtle airy texture. SFX: bottle cap twist, capsule shake, glass set down, sip of water, soft footsteps. Ambient kitchen morning tone remains quiet and natural.

Vertical 9:16 premium commercial product-only shot, no person on camera. Use the Bioluxe Lab NMN bottle as the hero with strict identity continuity from the image reference. Start on a polished stone countertop near a sunlit window, surrounded by minimal lifestyle cues: a clear water glass, a folded linen napkin, and a small ceramic bowl. Execute one smooth macro orbit around the bottle, then finish on a clean front-facing beauty angle with sharp label legibility. Lighting is bright morning sun with soft diffused fill, creating elegant highlights and a high-end wellness aesthetic. Keep the scene minimal, refined, and believable rather than glossy CGI. No text overlays, no extra products, no clutter. Product-only shot. No people, faces, or body parts visible. Realism directive: true-to-life commercial macro realism; physically accurate bottle geometry and label proportions, natural reflections/refractions and contact shadows, realistic material texture and micro-contrast, stable focus breathing and lens behavior, plausible camera motion, and authentic countertop/environment lighting with no CGI-like plastic sheen.
AUDIO: Calm trustworthy narration: "High-purity NMN from Bioluxe Lab, designed to support cellular health, energy metabolism, and healthy aging—one simple step, every morning. Shop now at bioluxelab.com." Soft premium piano resolves with a subtle warm swell. SFX: delicate bottle placement tap, faint room tone. Clean, polished, reassuring finish.
